title: DPIA Thresholds & Triggers
description: Criteria for determining when a Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) is mandatory. Verified against the JPDP DPIA Guideline issued 30 April 2026 (BM-only). PCP No. 1/2025 quantitative threshold for Automated Decision-Making was REMOVED in the issued guideline — ADM is now a qualitative factor (Q7).

guidelineStatus.issuedReference: Garis Panduan Penilaian Impak Perlindungan Data (DPIA), Jabatan Perlindungan Data Peribadi, April 2026
materialDivergencesFromPCP: ADM quantitative threshold removed; ADM moved to qualitative factor 7.6(g)
Sensitive personal data threshold explicitly extended to include financial information (maklumat kewangan)
Q2 Systematic Monitoring no longer limited to publicly accessible areas — broader scope including commercial settings and online behaviour
Commissioner-portal notification (PCP Para 2.18) removed — high residual risk reported to senior management; Commissioner gets on-request access only
DPIA validity period of 2 years introduced (must be redone, not just reviewed)
Retention is 2 years from cessation of processing operation (not from DPIA completion)

postDpiaObligations.implement: Implement the measures proposed in the DPIA to manage the specific risks identified.
postDpiaObligations.monitor: Monitor developments which may impact the processing operations and the risks (e.g., modified purposes, new vulnerabilities) per Section 11.3.
postDpiaObligations.retain: Retain DPIAs and supporting documentation for at least two (2) years FROM THE CESSATION of the processing operation (i.e., processing duration + 2 years; e.g., a 5-year processing operation requires 7 years of total retention). Records must be produced to the Commissioner upon request (Section 12.2).
postDpiaObligations.report: If overall residual risk is HIGH, the findings shall be reported to senior management of the data controller (Section 9). Senior management decides on additional mitigations, resource allocation, and acceptance of residual risk. The issued guideline does NOT impose Commissioner notification for high residual risk.

dpiaValidity.rule: A completed DPIA is valid for two (2) years from the date of completion (Section 11.2). Upon expiry, a new DPIA shall be conducted — not merely reviewed.
dpiaValidity.earlyTriggers: DPIA must be redone earlier than the 2-year cycle on any of the review triggers (material change to processing; new categories of data; new technology; data breach affecting the operation; regulatory guidance from JPDP).

mandate.primary: Processing operations LIKELY TO RESULT IN A HIGH RISK to the protection of personal data.

quantitative_thresholds.category: Sensitive Personal Data (including Biometric Data and Financial Information)
quantitative_thresholds.rationale: High impact due to data sensitivity. Issued Section 7.5 explicitly includes maklumat kewangan (financial information) within the sensitive-data trigger.
High impact due to scale of processing

removedFromQuantitative.rationale: PCP Para 2.10(b) proposed a quantitative threshold of >10,000 ADM data subjects. The issued guideline (Section 7.5) does NOT include this as a quantitative threshold. ADM is instead Qualitative Factor Q7.

qualitative_factors.description: Potential legal or significant effects on data subjects (e.g., noticeable impact on individuals' legal status/rights, financial status, health, reputation, or access to services).

Systematic monitoring of data subjects, including but not limited to publicly accessible areas, commercial settings (e.g., retail facial recognition), and online behaviour. The issued guideline broadened this from PCP's 'publicly accessible area' qualifier.
Use of fingerprint, face recognition, or other emerging/innovative technologies for enhanced access control or processing.
Processing that denies or restricts data subject rights (e.g., using automated decision-making to approve or reject loan/insurance applications).
Eye-tracking, location tracking, or generating data to provide tailored direct marketing.
Processing data of children or other vulnerable subjects to offer services.

Automated decision-making and profiling that may give rise to high risk to the data subject. Per issued Section 7.6(g) — moved from PCP's quantitative threshold into qualitative factors. DPO judgment required.

decision_logic: DPIA is REQUIRED if (a) any Quantitative Threshold is met OR (b) based on the DPO's best judgment, any Qualitative Factor suggests high risk.
